titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/220

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=168 status=live modified=2017-02-25 23:57:15.288796+00 | translation moa=169 status=live modified=2017-02-25 23:57:18.781864+00

div.p:
Norm’ vicecomes de Berewic . Omnibus hominibus suis de Crostorfin  salutem.
Sciatis me concessisse . ⁊ in perpetuam elemosinam dedisse . Sancte cruci de
Edeneb’ . ⁊ abbati . ⁊ fratribus ibidem deo seruientibus . Capellam meam de Cros
torfin cum omnibus rectitudinibus capelle eidem pertinentibus. Testibus . He[n]rico Comite
⁊ Edwardo Cancellario . ⁊ Turoldo archidiacono Lodonie . ⁊ Ricardo Cle
rico de Edeneb’. Apud Berewic

Norman, sheriff of Berwick, to all his men of Corstorphine, Greeting: Know that I have granted and given in perpetual alms to Holyrood in Edinburgh and to the abbot, and to the brethren serving God there, my Chapel at Corstorphine with all revenues belonging to the same chapel. As witnesses, Henry the Earl, and Edward the Chancellor, and Thurald the Archdeacon of Lothian, and Richard the Clerk of Edinburgh. At Berwick.
